description: Universal secret-material detection. The engine built-ins (sql-interpolation, private-key, hardcoded-credential) remain the always-on floor; this pack adds well-known token formats on top. Safe for any repo.

rules.message: AWS access key id committed — remove it, rotate the key, and load credentials from the environment / secret store
GitHub token committed — revoke it and load it from the environment / secret store
Slack token committed — revoke it and load it from the environment / secret store
private key block committed — remove it and rotate the key (pack mirror of the always-on built-in; also covers test fixtures)
password assigned a string literal — load it from the environment / secret store and rotate it

rules.//: Infrastructure metadata fields shouldn't appear in INFO-level log calls — log aggregators (CloudWatch, Datadog, Splunk) ship to third-party infra, and field names like 'table', 'arn', 'role_arn', 'bucket', 'endpoint', 'account_id' name production resources an attacker would otherwise have to enumerate. Acceptable: emit as DEBUG, or as a metric label (not in the log line). WARN severity — false-positive prone (e.g. an unrelated 'endpoint' field), so it surfaces for review rather than blocking.
rules.message: infrastructure metadata field name appears in an info-level log call — log aggregators ship to third-party infra; emit at debug-level instead, or expose as a metric label not a log field. Reviewed and accepted: add `# noqa: secrets-infra-metadata-in-info-log` (Python/Ruby/shell) or `//nolint:secrets-infra-metadata-in-info-log` (Go) at the end of the line, or move to a debug-level call.

rules.//: Companion to secrets-infra-metadata-in-info-log for multi-line structured log calls (e.g. slog.Info where each field is on its own line). Matches bare field-key string literals that name production infrastructure resources — these are the arguments that won't be caught by the single-line pattern above when the Info() call spans multiple lines. Same severity and suppression mechanism.
rules.message: infrastructure metadata field key on its own line — likely a multi-line structured log call; emit at debug-level instead or expose as a metric label. Reviewed and accepted: add `# noqa: secrets-infra-metadata-field-key` or `//nolint:secrets-infra-metadata-field-key` at the end of the line.
